conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you responsible for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and lives with me here in the UK. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with you in the UK.

To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask: **Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with him and are you responsible for paying for things like his food, clothes, and other living costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all his day-to-day needs - his food, clothes, school things, everything really. I'm his parent and I look after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That's helpful information.

Next, I need to ask: **Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex, such as his other parent or another family member?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Do you normally live in the UK? And are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, or do you have the right to reside in the UK through another route (such as EU settled status, refugee status, or a visa that permits you to claim benefits)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I normally live in the UK and I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility based on what you've told me.

Based on the information you've provided:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex and pay for his day-to-day needs (food, clothes, etc.)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- You normally live in the UK and are a UK citizen

Let me now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements:

- **Alex's age**: At 8 years old, he is under 16, which meets the age requirement
- **Responsibility**: You live with Alex and are responsible for his day-to-day needs (food, clothes, etc.)
- **Residence**: You normally live in the UK and are a UK citizen
- **No duplicate claims**: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You should be able to successfully apply for Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already applied, you can do so through the government's Child Benefit service.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is responsible for Alex, meaning they live with him and pay for his food, clothes and day-to-day needs. The claimant normally lives in the UK and is a UK citizen, meeting the residence and immigration status requirements. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict of claims. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for the child, live with him in the UK, are a UK citizen normally resident in the UK, and no one else is claiming for this child. The application should be successful.
